skill.name: video-enhancement
skill.description: AI Video Enhancement - Upscale video resolution, denoise, sharpen. Supports local files and YouTube/Bilibili URLs. HD/4K upscaling with real-time progress.
skill.summary: Upscale and enhance video quality using AI. Supports local files, YouTube, and Bilibili URLs with HD/4K output.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: Evaluation of 76 static findings found all critical and high severity items to be false positives in legitimate context. The skill is a video enhancement API client that invokes standard tools (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, curl) and makes API calls to verging.ai. The 'dangerous combination' heuristic triggered on the standard pattern of shell commands + network + API key, but no malicious intent was found. No prompt injection or obfuscation detected. The 'weak crypto' finding is a false positive triggered by API key format strings, not cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: External commands used for video processing
low_findings.description: The skill invokes yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl as documented shell commands. These are standard video processing tools, and usage is explicitly documented. No injection risk since commands use fixed tool names with parameterized arguments.

low_findings.title: Network access to verging.ai API endpoints
low_findings.description: All network access is to verging.ai API endpoints (auth, upload, job management). This is expected for a SaaS API client skill. No requests to unknown or suspicious domains.

low_findings.title: API key read from environment variable
low_findings.description: VERGING_API_KEY is read from environment, which is the recommended secure pattern for API key management. Key is passed via Authorization header to the API.

low_findings.title: Temporary directory usage
low_findings.description: Temp files stored in /tmp/verging-video-enhancement/ for intermediate video processing. Standard pattern for media processing. Documentation notes files are cleaned up after use.

content.user_title: Enhance and upscale videos with AI
content.value_statement: Transform low-resolution or noisy videos into sharp HD/4K output. This skill automates the full enhancement pipeline from local files or video URLs, delivering results without manual editing.
actual_capabilities: Upscale video resolution using AI models (normal or HD 4K mode)
Denoise and sharpen video frames for improved clarity
Download videos from YouTube and Bilibili URLs via yt-dlp
Trim videos to specified start and end times using ffmpeg
Process videos up to 30 seconds in duration (auto-trim longer)
Poll job status and return result URL when enhancement completes
limitations: Maximum video duration is 30 seconds; longer videos are auto-trimmed
Requires a verging.ai API key and account credits
HD mode costs 5 credits per second; normal mode costs 3 credits per second
Depends on external tools: yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl must be installed

use_cases.title: Content creator enhancing footage
use_cases.description: Improve the quality of recorded screencasts or short clips before publishing on social media or video platforms.

use_cases.title: Developer restoring old video clips
use_cases.description: Upscale and denoise archived or low-quality video files to modern HD/4K standards for presentations or documentation.

use_cases.title: Marketer preparing demo videos
use_cases.description: Enhance product demo videos from raw recordings into polished HD output ready for marketing campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance a local video file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-enhancement skill to upscale /path/to/recording.mp4 with denoising enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance a YouTube video with trimming
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-enhancement skill to process https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123, trim to seconds 10-25, and enable HD upscaling.

prompt_templates.title: HD 4K upscale with auto-download
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-enhancement skill to HD upscale /path/to/clip.mp4 and auto-download the result to the current directory.

prompt_templates.title: Batch process multiple videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-enhancement skill to process these local files: /path/to/video1.mp4, /path/to/video2.mp4. Apply HD mode and denoising to all.

output_examples.input: Enhance /tmp/recording.mp4 with HD upscaling and denoising.
output_examples.output: The skill downloads the file, uploads it to verging.ai, creates an HD enhancement job, polls until complete, and returns the result URL. The enhanced video is available at the provided download link, with 5 credits per second consumed.

output_examples.input: Process this YouTube URL with trimming to 15-30 seconds.
output_examples.output: The skill uses yt-dlp to download the video, trims it from 15 to 30 seconds using ffmpeg, uploads the trimmed clip, and submits an enhancement job. Result URL is returned with job status updates every 5 seconds.

output_examples.input: Check my remaining credits before enhancing.
output_examples.output: The skill calls the auth/me endpoint and returns your current credit balance. Normal mode consumes 3 credits per second; HD mode consumes 5 credits per second.

best_practices: Always verify you have sufficient credits before submitting HD jobs (5 credits/sec).
Trim longer videos to under 30 seconds before submission to avoid auto-trim behavior.
Store your API key in an environment variable rather than hardcoding it in scripts.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to process videos longer than 30 seconds without trimming first.
Avoid hardcoding API keys directly in shell commands or scripts.
Do not use this skill to enhance content you do not have rights to modify.

faq.question: What video sources are supported?
faq.answer: The skill accepts local video files (mp4 and other ffmpeg-supported formats) and URLs from YouTube and Bilibili. For other sources, download the file first and provide the local path.

faq.question: How much does enhancement cost?
faq.answer: Normal mode costs 3 credits per second. HD 4K mode costs 5 credits per second. Videos are processed at actual duration after trimming.

faq.question: What is the maximum video length?
faq.answer: Maximum processed duration is 30 seconds. Longer videos are automatically trimmed to the first 30 seconds unless you specify a shorter range with --start and --end.

faq.question: What tools need to be installed?
faq.answer: You need yt-dlp for downloading videos, ffmpeg and ffprobe for trimming and probing, and curl for API calls. All must be available in your system PATH.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and navigate to API Keys. Create a new key and set it as the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is my API key secure?
faq.answer: The skill reads the key from an environment variable, which is the recommended approach. Never commit keys to version control or expose them in shared scripts.
